answer:
To calculate the standard Gibbs energy of formation of \(\mathrm{Cl}^-(aq)\), we use the relationship between the standard cell potential \(E^\circ\) and the standard Gibbs energy change \(\Delta G^\circ\):

\[
\Delta G^\circ = -nFE^\circ
\]

For the given half-reaction \(\mathrm{AgCl}(s) + e^- \rightarrow \mathrm{Ag}(s) + \mathrm{Cl}^-(aq)\), the standard Gibbs energy change is also related to the standard Gibbs energies of formation:

\[
\Delta G^\circ = \Delta G_f^\circ(\mathrm{Cl}^-, aq) - \Delta G_f^\circ(\mathrm{AgCl}, s)
\]

Solving for \(\Delta G_f^\circ(\mathrm{Cl}^-, aq)\) with the given values (\(n = 1\), \(F = 96485 \, \mathrm{C/mol}\), \(E^\circ = +0.22
